page: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU scores and Passmark CPU ratings placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The hardware excels in memory bandwidth, achieving uncached large-block read speeds of 335.25 GB/sec. Specialized workloads such as ray tracing, image processing, and cryptographic operations, leveraging hardware-accelerated AES and SHA extensions, also register in the top 10%. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks sit within the average range.

As a bare-metal instance, c6id.metal offers high resource density by eliminating hypervisor overhead, making it highly resource-efficient for intensive parallel processing. The inclusion of 7.6 TB of high-speed local NVMe SSDs provides a cost-effective alternative to provisioning equivalent network-attached storage for IOPS-heavy workloads. This configuration is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and static web serving at scale.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs from dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 256 GB of DDR4 memory and a substantial 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high-speed uncached memory read bandwidth of 335.25 GB/sec, and excellent cryptographic and image processing capabilities. While single-core performance remains average, its multi-threaded capabilities and massive local storage make it highly efficient for parallel computing. The server is well-suited for high-performance computing, large-scale databases, software compilation, and intensive web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for high-performance workloads.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining 128 compute-optimized vCPUs with 7.6 TB of high-speed local NVMe storage for intensive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 3.5 GHz
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and uncached memory read performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Optimized for high-performance computing, databases, and software compilation
